text:
: وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: «۝ وَ مَنْ يُرِدْ فِيهِ بِإِلْحٰادٍ بِظُلْمٍ نُذِقْهُ مِنْ عَذٰابٍ أَلِيمٍ ۝»
قَالَ «كُلُّ ظُلْمٍ إِلْحَادٌ وَ ضَرْبُ اَلْخَادِمِ فِي غَيْرِ ذَنْبٍ مِنْ ذَلِكَ اَلْإِلْحَادِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2329 - He (Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar) said:
I asked him (Imam Abu Abdullah (as)) about the words of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic:
"And whoever intends a deviation therein or wrongdoing, We will make him taste a painful punishment." (Surah Al-Hajj 22:25)
Imam (as) replied: "Every act of injustice is a form of deviation (ilhad), and striking a servant without cause is among such deviation."
